text.title: Microservices Inter-Service Communication Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real microservice architectures.

antipattern.symptom: Service A → B → C → D sync chain; D slows down; A's p99 spikes 4x.
antipattern.root-cause: Cumulative sync coupling: each layer waits for the next; failures cascade.
antipattern.fix: Convert non-critical hops to async events. Add timeouts + circuit breakers on remaining sync hops. Where possible, denormalize so A doesn't need D.

antipattern.symptom: Adding a new consumer requires modifying the producer service to add another HTTP call.
antipattern.root-cause: Producer uses sync REST fan-out instead of publishing events.
antipattern.fix: Publish OrderCreated event to Kafka topic; consumers subscribe independently. Producer no longer knows consumer list.

antipattern.symptom: Producer adds optional field; consumer NPEs because their DTO doesn't know the field.
antipattern.root-cause: Schema defined in-code per side; no registry to enforce backward-compatibility checks.
antipattern.fix: Migrate contract to schema registry; CI runs compatibility check on every producer change.

antipattern.symptom: Retried POST creates a duplicate order; customer charged twice.
antipattern.root-cause: POST has no Idempotency-Key; server treats retries as new requests.
antipattern.fix: Server-side: hash (Idempotency-Key, body) → cache result for 24h. Client: generate UUID per logical request; reuse on retry.
